trigger_keywords: what headers are ignored by default
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The ignored headers are Cache-Control, Expires, Pragma, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, X-XSS-Protection. The sensitive headers are Authorization, Cookie, and Set-Cookie.
Tags are sorted alphabetically by their key value and joined into a single string using a comma followed by a space (e.g., 'key1=key1,key2=key2').
Each key=value pair is followed by a newline character. The response begins with the feature identifier (e.g., 'HTTP 200') or a specific label (e.g., 'service_id=GET').

context_pointers.actual_meaning: The URI's protocol component has a value other than 'https'
Application-level secure flag preference takes precedence over the server's global secure flag setting

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_route_mapping.json.description: Maps service identifiers into route paths when the identifier matches configured capture patterns, leaves unmatched identifiers unchanged, and cleans empty path segments from generated routes.

feature2_proxy_query_strings.json.description: Builds outbound query strings from ordered query parameters, preserving parameter order and rendering empty parameter values as bare parameter names.

feature3_proxy_request_uri_encoding.json.description: Builds the proxied request URI from the request context and percent-encodes non-ASCII path characters according to the request character encoding, while preserving an already encoded path.

feature4_proxy_header_and_response_handl.description: Copies inbound request headers into outbound proxy headers with all observed values, always requests gzip from upstream, and recognizes gzipped origin responses regardless of header-name casing.

cases.expected_output:
header=Accept-Encoding values=[gzip]
header=multiName values=[multiValue1, multiValue2]
header=singleName values=[singleValue]

feature5_secure_routing_decision.json.description: Determines whether an outbound client request should be treated as secure, giving an explicit client configuration precedence over discovered server metadata and upgrading an HTTP URI to HTTPS when the resolved route is secure.

feature6_metric_name_formatting.json.description: Formats a metric name as a dimensional identifier with all configured tags rendered inside parentheses in deterministic tag order.

feature7_gateway_header_defaults.json.description: Initializes gateway header filtering with built-in security-related ignored headers and sensitive headers, while allowing callers to replace global sensitive headers and route-specific sensitive headers.

cases.expected_output:
ignored_headers=[Cache-Control, Expires, Pragma, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, X-XSS-Protection]
sensitive_headers=[Authorization, Cookie, Set-Cookie]

ignored_headers=[Cache-Control, Expires, Pragma, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, X-XSS-Protection, x-foo]
sensitive_headers=[Authorization, Cookie, Set-Cookie]

route_sensitive_headers=[x-foo]
ignored_headers=[Cache-Control, Expires, Pragma, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, X-XSS-Protection]
sensitive_headers=[x-bar]

_constraints.output_rule: Generate route using routePattern with captured groups, removing empty path segments and trimming leading/trailing separators
Route remains exactly equal to serviceId

_constraints.input_condition: servicePattern matches but includes empty groups
_constraints.output_rule: Empty groups are filtered out during pattern expansion to prevent empty path segments
Output begins with '?', params rendered as name=value or name (if empty value)
Query parameters appear in the order defined in the input object
Rendered as parameter name only (no equals sign)
Request URI is output exactly as received (no re-encoding)
Output uses contextUri encoded via the specified characterEncoding (UTF-8)
Output all header values. Always include 'Accept-Encoding: gzip'. Preserve multi-valued headers as comma-separated lists within square brackets.
Detect gzip response regardless of header name casing. Set response_gzipped=true.
Use configuredSecure value only. Override serverSecure.
Use serverSecure value. Update uri scheme if secure=true and not already https.

_constraints.input_condition: resolved secure is true but uri is http
_constraints.output_rule: Change uri scheme from http to https
Output parentheses are empty
Render tags as key=value inside parentheses
Tags sorted alphabetically by key. Join with comma and space.
Use default ignored headers and default sensitive headers
exact_values: ignored_headers=[Cache-Control, Expires, Pragma, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, X-XSS-Protection]

_constraints.output_rule: Merge provided headers into default ignored list
Replace global sensitive list with provided list
Output route_sensitive_headers with provided list only
Key=Value pairs followed by newline. Output starts with Feature ID or specific label (e.g., 'service_id', 'query=')
